questions.text: In the context of legal issues in investigations, which of the following best defines 'spoliation of evidence'?
options: A. The intentional or negligent destruction, alteration, or concealment of evidence that is relevant to pending or reasonably anticipated litigation
B. The lawful retention of documents by a company for regulatory compliance purposes
C. The temporary suspension of document destruction policies during routine business operations
D. The voluntary disclosure of evidence to opposing counsel prior to formal discovery requests

questions.text: Which of the following best describes the legal doctrine of 'work product privilege' in the context of fraud investigations?
options: A. Protection for any documents created by employees during their regular job duties
B. Protection for materials prepared in anticipation of litigation by an attorney or under attorney direction
C. Immunity from prosecution for any statements made during internal investigations
D. A blanket exemption from document production for all investigative files

questions.text: In civil litigation discovery, what does the term 'relevant evidence' specifically mean under most jurisdictional standards?
options: A. Evidence that is highly probative and absolutely conclusive of all material facts
B. Evidence that directly contradicts the opposing party's claims exclusively
C. Evidence that has any tendency to make a fact of consequence more or less probable than it would be without the evidence
D. Evidence that is presented first during trial proceedings

questions.text: Which of the following accurately describes the concept of 'clawback provisions' in corporate legal contexts?
options: A. Provisions allowing companies to recover compensation from executives in cases of accounting restatements or misconduct
B. Automatic criminal penalties imposed by federal prosecutors
C. Rights granted to employees to retrieve deleted personal communications
D. Mandatory insurance requirements for all corporate officers

questions.text: In the context of fraud investigations, what is the primary legal distinction between 'civil fraud' and 'criminal fraud'?
options: A. Civil fraud requires proof beyond a reasonable doubt, while criminal fraud requires only preponderance of evidence
B. Criminal fraud requires proof beyond a reasonable doubt and typically involves intent to deceive, while civil fraud requires preponderance of evidence and may not require criminal intent
C. There is no meaningful legal distinction between the two standards
D. Civil fraud cases are always resolved faster than criminal fraud cases

questions.text: Which of the following best defines 'adverse inference' in the context of litigation and evidence rules?
options: A. A negative conclusion drawn by a fact-finder when a party fails to produce evidence that was in their control
B. A statement made by an attorney that is binding on all future cases
C. An automatic dismissal of claims due to procedural violations
D. A privilege that prevents any adverse comments about a witness's character

questions.text: In fraud investigations, what does the legal term 'reasonable suspicion' require, as distinguished from 'probable cause'?
options: A. Reasonable suspicion requires the same quantum of evidence as probable cause
B. Reasonable suspicion requires more specific evidence than probable cause
C. Reasonable suspicion requires a lower threshold of particularized and objective basis for suspecting illegal activity, while probable cause requires substantially more evidence
D. Reasonable suspicion applies only to criminal matters, never to civil investigations

questions.text: Which of the following best describes what constitutes 'materiality' in the context of securities fraud investigations?
options: A. Any information that could potentially affect a company's stock price by any measurable amount
B. Information that a reasonable investor would consider important in making an investment decision
C. Only information that results in actual quantifiable financial losses to investors
D. Information that is disclosed in footnotes rather than in the main body of financial statements

questions.text: In the context of investigations, how does 'qualified immunity' affect the liability of corporate security personnel or investigators?
options: A. It provides absolute protection from all civil liability for any investigative actions
B. It protects government officials from civil liability when performing discretionary functions, but generally does not apply to private sector investigators
C. It automatically covers all employees of corporations engaged in internal investigations
D. It eliminates the need for investigators to obtain proper authorization before conducting interviews

questions.text: Which of the following best defines 'hearsay' under the Federal Rules of Evidence and why it is typically restricted in legal proceedings?
options: A. Any statement made by a witness during an investigation, regardless of reliability
B. An out-of-court statement offered to prove the truth of the matter asserted, restricted because the declarant is unavailable for cross-examination
C. Any testimony given by a witness who has a financial interest in the outcome
D. Written statements that are longer than one paragraph in length

questions.text: In fraud investigations, what is the legal significance of the 'business records exception' to the hearsay rule?
options: A. It eliminates the need for any documentary evidence in corporate investigations
B. It allows records made in the regular course of business to be admitted even though they are hearsay, provided foundational requirements are met
C. It automatically renders all corporate records inadmissible in litigation
D. It requires all business records to be certified by external auditors before use in investigations

questions.text: Which of the following accurately describes the legal concept of 'fiduciary duty' in the context of corporate fraud investigations?
options: A. A duty that exists only between external auditors and shareholders
B. A legal obligation requiring officers, directors, and key employees to act in the best interests of the company and its shareholders, breaches of which can form the basis for legal claims
C. A requirement that all employees report directly to the board of directors
D. An insurance requirement rather than a legal obligation

questions.text: In the context of legal holds and litigation readiness, what does the term 'chain of custody' specifically ensure?
options: A. That documents remain in a secure locked cabinet at all times
B. That the authenticity and integrity of evidence is maintained through documented tracking of its possession and handling from collection through presentation
C. That only senior management can access investigative files
D. That evidence is automatically admissible in court proceedings
questions.tag: Chain of Custody and Evidence Integrity

questions.text: When a fraud examiner discovers that a witness has provided contradictory statements in two separate interviews, which legal principle most directly affects the admissibility and weight of such testimony in potential litigation?
options: A. The doctrine of equitable estoppel, which prevents witnesses from changing their accounts
B. The principle of impeachment, which allows credibility to be challenged through inconsistent prior statements
C. The rule against hearsay, which renders all prior statements inadmissible regardless of contradiction
D. The doctrine of res judicata, which prevents re-examination of witness testimony once given

questions.text: A CFE must distinguish between spoliation of evidence and mere negligent loss of documents during an investigation. Which of the following factors is MOST critical in making this legal distinction?
options: A. The monetary value of the documents destroyed or lost
B. The intent to destroy evidence combined with knowledge of pending or reasonably anticipated litigation
C. The physical location where the documents were stored at the time of loss
D. The number of copies of the documents that existed in different locations

questions.text: In contrast to administrative investigations, criminal investigations conducted by law enforcement agents are subject to heightened legal standards regarding which of the following?
options: A. The requirement to document interview summaries in writing
B. Constitutional protections such as the Fifth Amendment right against self-incrimination when custodial interrogation occurs
C. The obligation to preserve all documents collected during the investigation
D. The duty to disclose all witness identities to the subject under investigation

questions.text: A company's outside counsel instructs investigators not to document certain preliminary investigative steps in writing to preserve attorney-client privilege. How should a CFE respond to this instruction?
options: A. Comply fully, as outside counsel's instructions supersede the CFE's professional obligations
B. Decline to participate in undocumented investigation activities, as this approach may actually waive privilege and violate the CFE Code of Professional Ethics
C. Document activities only with counsel's written approval to avoid any appearance of misconduct
D. Create separate documented and undocumented investigation tracks to accommodate counsel's request
questions.tag: Privilege and Professional Ethics in Documentation

questions.text: When distinguishing between a permissible internal investigation interview and an unlawful interrogation that violates labor law, which element is NOT a determining factor?
options: A. Whether the employee was explicitly told they could terminate the interview and leave
B. Whether the questioning occurred in an isolated location without union representation when required
C. The personal educational background and professional experience of the interviewer
D. Whether the interview was coercive in nature or the employee reasonably felt they could not refuse to participate
questions.tag: Lawful Interview Protocols and Labor Law

questions.text: A CFE working on behalf of a company discovers evidence of fraud that also implicates a violation of federal securities law. Which legal obligation takes precedence regarding the duty to report?
options: A. The company's internal reporting policy controls the timing and nature of disclosure
B. The CFE's professional duty to the company prevents any external disclosure without authorization
C. Statutory and regulatory obligations (such as mandatory disclosure requirements) may supersede contractual confidentiality with the employer
D. The CFE must await direction from the company's board of directors before any reporting consideration

questions.text: How does the legal concept of 'work product doctrine' differ from attorney-client privilege when protecting investigation materials?
options: A. Work product doctrine is broader and applies to all documents created during any investigation phase
B. Work product doctrine protects materials prepared in anticipation of litigation regardless of attorney involvement, while privilege requires attorney involvement and confidential communication
C. Work product doctrine requires that materials be created at the attorney's explicit direction, unlike privilege
D. Work product doctrine provides stronger protection because it has no exceptions for crime-fraud situations
questions.tag: Work Product Doctrine and Privilege Distinction

questions.text: When a CFE obtains evidence through a method that is legal in the investigative context but may be ethically questionable, what is the proper course of action?
options: A. Use the evidence without hesitation since legal permissibility is the only relevant standard
B. Consult the CFE Code of Professional Ethics and potentially seek counsel advice before proceeding, recognizing that legal and ethical standards may diverge
C. Immediately disclose the method to the subject of the investigation to ensure transparency
D. Exclude the evidence from the investigation report while preserving it for potential law enforcement use

questions.text: A company receives a civil litigation hold notice before any criminal investigation begins. How does this timing difference affect the CFE's document preservation obligations?
options: A. Civil hold notices create no binding preservation obligations; only criminal investigation notices matter
B. The CFE must preserve materials from the civil hold notice date forward, and the subsequent criminal investigation creates additional obligations for documents predating the civil notice
C. Criminal investigation holds automatically void previous civil preservation requirements
D. Both civil and criminal holds require identical preservation scopes with no differentiation
questions.tag: Timing of Legal Holds and Preservation Scope

questions.text: Which distinction is most important when determining whether a CFE should conduct voluntary interviews versus compulsory interviews during a workplace investigation?
options: A. The geographic location of the witness relative to company headquarters
B. Whether the interviewee is represented by counsel and the jurisdiction's laws regarding voluntary participation in employer investigations
C. The seniority level of the person being interviewed within the organization
D. The amount of evidence already collected before the interview occurs

questions.text: A CFE discovers that surveillance footage used to support investigation findings was obtained without proper authorization under state privacy law. What is the most appropriate response?
options: A. Continue using the evidence since it was already obtained and cannot be 'un-obtained'
B. Disclose the legal issue to appropriate stakeholders and seek guidance, as the evidence may be inadmissible and its use could expose the company to liability
C. Rely on the IT department's assertion that the surveillance was lawful and continue the investigation
D. Use the evidence internally but exclude it from any external reporting to avoid complications

questions.text: When comparing the legal standards for obtaining a confession in a corporate fraud investigation versus a police interrogation, which statement most accurately reflects the distinction?
options: A. Corporate investigators have more stringent requirements because they lack police authority

B. Police must observe Miranda protections for custodial interrogation, while corporate investigators have greater latitude absent governmental action, but both must avoid coercion that would render statements involuntary
C. Corporate confessions are automatically inadmissible regardless of how they were obtained
D. Police confessions require less corroboration than corporate confessions due to law enforcement expertise
questions.tag: Confession Standards: Corporate vs. Police Interrogation

questions.text: A CFE is asked to investigate potential contract fraud involving a third-party vendor. Which legal consideration most significantly differentiates this investigation from an employee fraud case?
options: A. Third-party investigations never require document preservation planning

B. The vendor is not subject to the company's internal investigation authority, and privacy rights and contractual obligations between the company and vendor govern what investigation steps are permissible
C. All investigative techniques available for employee investigations are equally available for vendor investigations without modification
D. Third-party investigations cannot involve interviews under any circumstance due to lack of employment relationship

questions.text: A fraud examiner discovers that a company's outside counsel has been providing legal advice to management regarding potential settlement of a fraud case. During the investigation, the examiner requests access to all communications between counsel and management. What is the most appropriate response?
options: A. All communications must be disclosed because the company waived privilege by conducting an internal investigation
B. Communications are protected by attorney-client privilege only if made primarily for the purpose of obtaining legal advice, not business advice
C. The examiner can access the communications with management approval, as privilege belongs to the corporation
D. All documents must be produced because privilege does not apply to settlement discussions

questions.text: During a workplace investigation, a manager discovers that an employee has been selling company confidential information. The manager prepares a detailed report documenting the evidence and shares it with the company's human resources department, finance team, and board audit committee. Which party's receipt of this report is most likely to waive attorney-client privilege if the report was prepared at counsel's direction?
options: A. Sharing with the human resources department only
B. Sharing with the finance team in addition to HR
C. Sharing with individuals not present for legal advice purposes
D. Sharing with the board audit committee as part of governance oversight

questions.text: A CFE is investigating embezzlement at a nonprofit organization. During the investigation, the CFE discovers that the suspect employee's spouse has relevant financial information stored on a shared family computer at their home. The CFE wants to obtain this information. Which statement best describes the legal issues involved?
options: A. The information can be obtained freely because the organization owns the data
B. The CFE must obtain a subpoena because the employee has a reasonable expectation of privacy in their home
C. The CFE can request the information from the employer's IT system only
D. The information is protected by spousal privilege and cannot be obtained in any circumstance

questions.text: Following a fraud investigation that resulted in criminal referrals, the company receives a subpoena from law enforcement requesting all investigative files, interview notes, and work product prepared by the company's outside counsel. The company's counsel asserts attorney-client privilege and work product protection. Which documents are most likely to be protected from disclosure?
options: A. Interview notes with witnesses regarding factual observations of the fraud
B. Email communications between counsel and management discussing litigation strategy
C. Financial records and transaction documentation obtained during the investigation
D. Preliminary factual findings documented before counsel was retained
questions.tag: Work Product Doctrine and Legal Privilege

questions.text: A company's fraud examination team plans to conduct interviews with employees regarding suspected kickback schemes involving vendors. Before conducting these interviews, which action would most likely compromise the company's ability to claim privilege over subsequent attorney-client communications?
options: A. Having outside counsel present during all interviews
B. Documenting that interviews are being conducted at management's direction for business purposes only
C. Conducting preliminary interviews before retaining counsel, then sharing all findings with counsel
D. Requesting that interviewed employees maintain confidentiality of the investigation

questions.text: During an investigation into potential securities fraud, a CFE discovers that a company executive destroyed emails after receiving notice of the investigation. The CFE wants to pursue sanctions against the executive. Which legal doctrine would most directly support pursuing sanctions for document destruction?
options: A. The doctrine of spoliation, which addresses destruction of evidence after notice of litigation
B. The attorney-client privilege doctrine, which protects communications with counsel
C. The work product protection doctrine, which shields investigative materials
D. The business records exception, which governs admissibility of corporate documents

questions.text: A CFE is investigating payroll fraud at a mid-sized manufacturing company. The company's IT department discovers evidence of fraud on the company's server. The suspected fraudster's attorney demands that all investigation materials be immediately destroyed under attorney-client privilege. What is the company's best response?
options: A. Comply with the attorney's demand to destroy all materials to maintain privilege
B. Distinguish between materials created with counsel's direction for legal advice and factual information about the fraud
C. Immediately produce all materials to law enforcement to avoid spoliation concerns
D. Halt the investigation pending resolution of the privilege claim in court

questions.text: A company investigates a manager suspected of approving fraudulent invoices from a fictitious vendor. During interviews, several employees provide conflicting accounts of who authorized the payments. The company's counsel advises that detailed interview summaries be prepared. Which party should review and approve these summaries before they are finalized to best preserve privilege?
options: A. The employees who were interviewed, to ensure accuracy
B. Outside counsel who directed the investigation, not management or HR
C. The company's audit committee or board, for governance purposes
D. Law enforcement, if criminal referral is contemplated
questions.tag: Control and Direction in Privileged Investigations

questions.text: During a fraud investigation, a CFE uncovers evidence that a company officer received undisclosed payments from a major customer. The officer's personal attorney contacts the CFE and asserts that certain documents are protected by the officer's personal attorney-client privilege. Under which circumstance would the officer's privilege claim most likely prevail against the company's investigation?
options: A. When the officer retained personal counsel before providing any statements to the company
B. When communications relate to the officer's potential personal criminal liability separate from corporate interests
C. When the officer was interviewed by company counsel without being informed of investigation purposes
D. When the officer requested counsel's presence during all investigative interviews

questions.text: A CFE discovers that a company's controller has been manipulating revenue recognition to meet quarterly targets. The company's outside counsel asks the CFE to prepare a detailed analysis comparing the controller's current accounting practices to GAAP requirements and recommending corrective actions. How should the CFE best document this analysis to maintain privilege protection?
options: A. Submit the analysis directly to the company's finance team for implementation
B. Prepare the analysis as a memorandum to counsel that is clearly marked as confidential legal advice
C. Distribute the analysis widely to ensure all relevant departments understand the findings
D. Document the analysis as a factual report separate from any attorney communications

questions.text: A company self-reports a fraud matter to the SEC and subsequently receives a document preservation notice from federal regulators. The company's counsel and CFE team develop an investigation plan. What is the primary legal consequence if the company fails to preserve relevant documents after receiving notice of potential litigation or investigation?
options: A. Automatic criminal prosecution of responsible executives
B. Potential sanctions including adverse inference instructions, fines, or case dismissal
C. Loss of attorney-client privilege over all investigative materials
D. Mandatory referral to law enforcement for criminal investigation
questions.tag: Legal Consequences of Non-Compliance with Preservation Obligations

questions.text: During a complex financial fraud investigation, a CFE and outside counsel jointly interview a key witness. At the conclusion of the interview, the witness's personal attorney objects and claims the interview violated the witness's rights. Which statement best describes the legal implications for privilege protection of this jointly-conducted interview?
options: A. The interview is not privileged because the witness was not represented by the company's counsel
B. The interview may be privileged only as to communications made in the witness's presence with counsel directing the interview
C. Privilege cannot be asserted over witness testimony obtained during joint interviews with company counsel present
D. The witness can waive privilege by objecting after the interview occurred

questions.text: A CFE is retained to investigate potential bribery in a company's international operations. The investigation uncovers evidence that a company subsidiary in Country X may have violated local laws. The CFE's report includes recommendations for legal remediation prepared at counsel's direction. A foreign government requests access to the investigation report. Which principle most directly determines whether the CFE must disclose the report to the foreign government?
options: A. The laws of Country X regarding privilege protection and document disclosure obligations
B. U.S. attorney-client privilege law applies universally to all company subsidiaries
C. The company's choice of whether to claim privilege or conduct transparent operations
D. International agreements that automatically extend U.S. privilege to foreign proceedings
questions.tag: Privilege in Cross-Border and International Investigations

questions.text: A CFE is investigating potential embezzlement at a multinational corporation with operations in multiple jurisdictions. During the investigation, the examiner discovers that relevant documents are stored in countries with strict data protection laws that prohibit cross-border transfer without explicit consent. What is the most appropriate legal approach to address this jurisdictional conflict?
options: A. Immediately transfer all documents to the home country to ensure unified investigation control
B. Engage local counsel in each jurisdiction to understand applicable data protection laws and obtain necessary consents or legal authorizations before document transfer
C. Request that the company's IT department anonymize all data before transfer to circumvent data protection restrictions
D. Proceed with document collection under the assumption that internal corporate investigations supersede local privacy laws
questions.tag: Cross-Border Data Protection and Jurisdictional Compliance

questions.text: During a workplace investigation into alleged kickback schemes, a CFE obtains privileged communications between company counsel and management discussing investigation strategy. The CFE inadvertently includes these documents in the evidence file provided to external auditors. Which consequence is most likely to result from this inadvertent disclosure?
options: A. Waiver of attorney-client privilege for all communications related to the investigation
B. Potential waiver of privilege limited to the specifically disclosed documents, though the scope depends on whether the privilege holder took prompt remedial measures
C. Complete immunity from liability because the disclosure was inadvertent rather than intentional
D. Automatic disqualification of the CFE from continuing the investigation due to privilege breach
questions.tag: Inadvertent Privilege Waiver and Remedial Measures

questions.text: A fraud examiner is preparing to conduct interviews with multiple employees as part of a suspected fraudulent billing scheme investigation. One employee requests union representation before being interviewed. Which of the following best describes the legal obligation regarding this request?
options: A. The request must be honored under the National Labor Relations Act (NLRA) Weingarten rights if the employee is unionized and reasonably believes the interview could result in disciplinary action
B. The request can be denied because internal fraud investigations are exempt from union representation requirements
C. The CFE must immediately contact the union to request permission before proceeding with the interview
D. The employee's presence alone satisfies the requirement; union representation is optional and at company discretion
questions.tag: Employee Representation Rights and Weingarten Principles

questions.text: A company's investigation reveals that a senior finance executive used company resources to conduct personal business, diverting approximately $250,000 over three years. The executive's employment contract includes a clawback provision for misconduct-related compensation. Before taking disciplinary action, what is the primary legal consideration the company should address?
options: A. Ensure the clawback provision complies with applicable securities laws and does not violate state employment law restrictions on compensation recovery
B. Simply enforce the clawback provision as written in the contract without additional legal review
C. Offer the executive a settlement to avoid costly litigation over clawback enforcement
D. Immediately freeze the executive's bank accounts to secure assets before the clawback provision is enforced
questions.tag: Clawback Provisions and Executive Compensation Recovery

questions.text: A CFE is investigating suspected intellectual property theft where an employee is suspected of downloading proprietary trade secrets before termination. The examiner wants to examine the employee's computer and email accounts. Which legal framework should primarily govern the scope and legality of this examination?
options: A. The Computer Fraud and Abuse Act (CFAA), which broadly permits employers to access any employee device without restriction
B. State wiretapping and electronic surveillance laws, combined with company policies regarding employee monitoring and consent at hire
C. Federal Trade Commission (FTC) guidelines that require explicit written consent before any digital forensics
D. International data protection treaties that supersede all domestic employment law considerations
questions.tag: Digital Forensics and Electronic Device Examination Authority

questions.text: During a fraud investigation, a CFE interviews a witness who provides relevant information but expresses concern about retaliation from the suspect employee who remains actively employed. What is the most legally appropriate response to address both the investigation's needs and legal protection of the witness?
options: A. Assure the witness that retaliation is illegal and retaliation concerns are not the investigator's responsibility

B. Document the retaliation concern, advise the witness of applicable anti-retaliation protections under law, coordinate with HR regarding interim protective measures, and maintain confidentiality to the extent possible
C. Immediately suspend the suspect employee to demonstrate commitment to witness protection
D. Require the witness to sign a non-disclosure agreement forbidding discussion of retaliation fears to prevent spreading rumors

questions.text: A company receives a subpoena from a federal grand jury requesting documents and communications related to a suspected fraud investigation. The company's counsel advises that some requested documents contain attorney work product from the company's outside counsel. What is the appropriate legal response?
options: A. Produce all documents without reservation, as grand jury subpoenas cannot be challenged or limited
B. File a motion to quash the subpoena entirely to prevent any document disclosure
C. Produce responsive documents while asserting a work product doctrine claim for protected materials, providing a privilege log that describes withheld documents and the basis for the assertion
D. Destroy work product documents before the subpoena deadline to avoid producing protected materials
questions.tag: Subpoena Compliance and Work Product Doctrine Assertion

questions.text: A CFE discovers during an investigation that the company's Chief Financial Officer has been making false representations to auditors regarding the collectability of receivables. The CFE reports findings to the Audit Committee, which directs the examiner not to disclose the matter to external auditors or regulators. Which legal principle most directly conflicts with this directive?
options: A. The company's duty to maintain confidentiality of internal investigation findings from all external parties
B. The auditor's independent responsibility to obtain sufficient evidence regarding material accounts, combined with potential regulatory reporting obligations under securities laws
C. The CFE's professional obligation to protect the privacy of all interviewed witnesses regardless of findings
D. The principle of legal privilege that prevents any information sharing beyond the immediate investigation team
questions.tag: Auditor Communication and Regulatory Disclosure Obligations

questions.text: A company initiates an investigation into possible contract fraud and discovers that relevant evidence exists in emails deleted by the suspected employee. The company's IT department indicates that recovery is technically possible but expensive. Before authorizing the recovery effort, what legal consideration should guide the decision?
options: A. The company can recover deleted emails without limitation because they are company property
B. The relevance and materiality of the deleted emails to the investigation must be weighed against privacy expectations and proportionality, considering available alternative evidence
C. Deleted emails are permanently destroyed and cannot be recovered under any circumstances
D. Recovery should proceed automatically regardless of cost because all deleted company emails are potential evidence
questions.tag: Proportionality in Evidence Recovery and Preservation

questions.text: A CFE is investigating potential bribery of government officials by company representatives. During the investigation, the examiner learns that the company's outside counsel intends to self-report the matter to the Department of Justice under the Foreign Corrupt Practices Act (FCPA) Pilot Program. What is the legal significance of this timing for the ongoing investigation?
options: A. The self-reporting negates the need for continued investigation because the company has already disclosed the violation
B. The investigation should be suspended immediately to avoid interfering with the DOJ's prosecutorial discretion
C. The investigation may continue, but the timing and scope should be coordinated with counsel to support the self-reporting disclosure and ensure consistency of facts presented to the DOJ
D. The CFE must immediately report directly to the DOJ independently of the company's counsel reporting process
questions.tag: Self-Reporting Coordination and Regulatory Agency Interaction

questions.text: A CFE interviews a suspect employee who, when confronted with evidence of misconduct, requests to speak with an attorney before answering further questions. The examiner is not a law enforcement officer. What is the legally appropriate response?
options: A. Immediately terminate the interview and provide the employee with a list of recommended attorneys
B. Continue questioning to obtain the employee's statement before the attorney arrives, as Miranda protections do not apply to private employers
C. Terminate the interview, respect the employee's request, and document that the employee invoked the right to counsel before further statements were obtained
D. Contact the company's legal department to force the employee to waive the attorney request before proceeding
questions.tag: Right to Counsel Invocation in Private Sector Interviews

questions.text: A company discovers that an employee has been engaged in patent infringement through the unauthorized use and sale of company intellectual property to competitors. The company wants to pursue both civil remedies and criminal referral. What legal framework should primarily govern the selection of remedies and their sequencing?
options: A. Civil and criminal remedies can be pursued simultaneously without any legal coordination concerns
B. Criminal referral must be made first, before any civil action, to maintain the integrity of criminal prosecution
C. Civil remedies should be prioritized and pursued first, with criminal referral only if civil litigation fails

D. The company should coordinate with legal counsel regarding potential conflicts between civil discovery and criminal proceedings, consider the strategic implications of each, and sequence actions to maximize protection of both civil and criminal interests
questions.tag: Concurrent Civil and Criminal Remedy Strategy

questions.text: A CFE preparing to present investigation findings to the company's Board of Directors learns that one Board member is a close family relative of the suspect employee. The Board member requests to review the investigation report before the full Board presentation. What is the most legally and ethically appropriate approach?
options: A. Provide the report to the Board member as requested because Board members have automatic access to all company information
B. Deny the request entirely and refuse to discuss any findings with individual Board members prior to full Board presentation

C. Consult with the Audit Committee Chair and company counsel regarding potential conflicts of interest and confidentiality concerns, and follow their guidance on appropriate disclosure and participation restrictions
D. Provide the report but require the Board member to sign a non-disclosure agreement prohibiting discussion with the suspect employee
questions.tag: Board Conflicts of Interest and Confidential Reporting Governance
